---
title: "February 24th, 2026"
---

## Summary

Google Earth Engine (GEE) is a free (for now and for certain limited purposes) platform that serves and displays data from EO instruments programmatically, as well as providing some cloud-computing resources and built-in functions for common operations which are optimized to take advantage of running on Google's servers. Scripting in GEE requires Javascript, which is less familiar to me than Python, but also less annoying to me than R. I'll take it. According to today's lecture, LLMs are not very familiar with GEE code, since there is so much more vanilla Javascript for them to have trained on than GEE-specific code. 

Interestingly, there is some interplay between the GUI and the scripting, allowing a user to draw in polygons and select points using the mouse. 

The main task of the practical was combining multiple tiles into a mosaiced image using the median to combine multiple passes of the same tile, and the mean to combine the overlapping portions of images. Why not use something spatial to combine them, like at least an interpolation between tiles through the overlapping portion? We also reviewed band math, texture measures, and PCA using GEE. PCA in particular was much more involved than in R or Python, but not insurmountable to understand with some careful reading.

## Applications



## Reflection
